entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: Cultivated groundnut (Arachis hypogaea), a tetraploid species composed of A and B genomes

entities.name: GENOME A
entities.description: One of the two genomes comprising the cultivated groundnut

entities.name: GENOME B
entities.description: One of the two genomes comprising the cultivated groundnut

entities.name: POLYPLOIDIZATION
entities.description: The process of increasing the number of sets of chromosomes that gave rise to tetraploid groundnut

entities.name: A. DURANENSIS
entities.description: A diploid species identified as the A genome donor of Arachis hypogaea

entities.name: A. BATIZOCOI
entities.description: A species proposed as the B genome donor of Arachis hypogaea

entities.name: A. IPAENSIS
entities.description: A species suggested as the B genome donor of Arachis hypogaea based on RFLP and karyotype studies

entities.name: A. HOEHNEI
entities.description: A species identified as the probable B genome donor of Arachis hypogaea

entities.name: RFLP
entities.description: Restriction fragment length polymorphism used to suggest A. ipaensis as the B genome donor

entities.name: KARYOTYPE STUDIES
entities.description: Studies of chromosome number and appearance used to support A. duranensis and A. ipaensis as donors

entities.name: CROSSABILITY
entities.description: The ability of two different species to be crossed, used to study A. hoehnei

entities.name: CYTOGENETICAL STUDIES
entities.description: Studies of chromosomes and their relation to cell behavior, used to study A. hoehnei

entities.name: MOLECULAR STUDIES
entities.description: Studies of molecular biology used to study A. hoehnei

entities.name: B GENOME DONOR
entities.description: The ancestral diploid species that contributed the B genome to the tetraploid groundnut
